creator: ohartmann@walstatt.org
text:
Thanks for the response.

The Wiki mentioned in the initial PR has been reported to me when I asked for help/howto to move away from the Arduino 1.8.6 IDE towards a Makefile based environment. I'm new to such kind of development, I did my work mostly with make/automake and that on a very limited basis.

To use the Arduino IDE (devel/arduino18) the port devel/extensa-esp32-elf is a prerequisite. Arduino IDE supports ESP32 by cloning/copying the arduino-esp32 sources into the sketchbook pathway of a user-based development folder and refelct the location in the setup of the IDE. So I have

$HOME/projects/arduino.git/sketchbook/hardware/espressif/esp32 

containing the necessary sources I need. I'm unaware whether I need the ESP-IDF provided by the same repo or even the esptool repo, if using Arduino IDE.

I do not know how the Arduino IDE is determining the IDE necessary, when I compile, I always see that ESP-IDF 4.4-beta is used, no matter what ESP32 sources I checked out so far (mostly V4.X via tags/v4.x.x, did not try V3.x).

To make it short: I would appreciate a more modern xtensa compiler (ESP-IDF doesn#t work for freeBSD natively, but the install.sh script seems to reel in the 2021r1 version based on gcc8), since there is support fpr ESP32-C3 which is suposed to be RISC-V based. I have no clue whether LLVM/CLANG is going to support ESP32 and its RISC-V sibbling in a sufficient way so one can omit the gcc version.
